title: We are told, ’’At daybreak, He called together his disciples and chose twelve of them to be his Apostles.’ The disciples became apostles. For these disciples, ‘Apostles’  was what God wanted them to become, and what God wanted them to do. Their call to be ‘Apostles needed to be rooted in discipleship. It is the same for us, we will not be the people God wants us to be, or do the things God wants us to do, without a life of discipleship.
scripture_ref: ‘’One of those days Jesus went out to a mountainside to pray, and spent the night praying to God. When morning came, he called his disciples to him and chose twelve of them, whom he also designated apostles: Simon (whom he named Peter), his brother Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Simon who was called the Zealot, Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.’’
scripture_text: 4. God purposes and goals for you life is rooted in discipleship.
devotional:
It is very interesting that we would refer to ourselves as Christians before we would see ourselves as disciples. The name Christian, is only found 3 times in the bible, (Acts 11:25-26, Acts 26:28, 1 Peter 4:16).

What’s interesting about all three of these occurrences is that the word “Christian” as a title or identifier doesn’t seem to be self-applied. Rather, it is non-Christians, unbelievers, people who do not follow Jesus, who are using the term to describe or identify Jesus’ disciples or followers.

The word disciple is found 261 times in the Gospels and Acts, 297 times in the New Testament. When it come to understanding our part, or role in our spiritual journey and maturity, we need to think and behave like disciples.

Dallas Williard said ‘His disciples were with him learning to be like him.’ This truly captures the essence of discipleship. Transformation because of authentic fellowship with Jesus.

We have said nothing about the ‘twelve’ that Jesus called to be his apostles. But I wonder what our lives would be like if our names were in that list. Jesus will never call us to be capital A ‘Apostles’ like these disciples, but we can be sure of three things.

a. Jesus will call you to something. You will never be a spectator or a passenger, every disciple has the call of God upon their lives.

b. The call of God grows from a life of discipleship, being with him, being like him.

c. Discipleship equips us for the will of God. Everything we have been called to has apostolic significance because we are emissaries of THE GREAT KING.
prayer: Prayer, Father help us to live lives that are so close to Jesus that we will be able to serve others as He would.
html:
We are told, ’’At daybreak, He called together his disciples and chose twelve of them to be his Apostles.’ The disciples became apostles. For these disciples, ‘Apostles’ was what God wanted them to become, and what God wanted them to do. Their call to be ‘Apostles needed to be rooted in discipleship. It is the same for us, we will not be the people God wants us to be, or do the things God wants us to do, without a life of discipleship.

‘’One of those days Jesus went out to a mountainside to pray, and spent the night praying to God. When morning came, he called his disciples to him and chose twelve of them, whom he also designated apostles: Simon (whom he named Peter), his brother Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Simon who was called the Zealot, Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.’’
4. God purposes and goals for you life is rooted in discipleship.

It is very interesting that we would refer to ourselves as Christians before we would see ourselves as disciples. The name Christian, is only found 3 times in the bible, (Acts 11:25-26, Acts 26:28, 1 Peter 4:16).

What’s interesting about all three of these occurrences is that the word “Christian” as a title or identifier doesn’t seem to be self-applied. Rather, it is non-Christians, unbelievers, people who do not follow Jesus, who are using the term to describe or identify Jesus’ disciples or followers.

The word disciple is found 261 times in the Gospels and Acts, 297 times in the New Testament. When it come to understanding our part, or role in our spiritual journey and maturity, we need to think and behave like disciples.

Dallas Williard said ‘His disciples were with him learning to be like him.’ This truly captures the essence of discipleship. Transformation because of authentic fellowship with Jesus.

We have said nothing about the ‘twelve’ that Jesus called to be his apostles. But I wonder what our lives would be like if our names were in that list. Jesus will never call us to be capital A ‘Apostles’ like these disciples, but we can be sure of three things.

a. Jesus will call you to something. You will never be a spectator or a passenger, every disciple has the call of God upon their lives.

b. The call of God grows from a life of discipleship, being with him, being like him.

c. Discipleship equips us for the will of God. Everything we have been called to has apostolic significance because we are emissaries of THE GREAT KING.

Prayer: Prayer, Father help us to live lives that are so close to Jesus that we will be able to serve others as He would.
Bible in a year reading plan: - Joshua 18:1-21:12
